commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the stable-pin runtime-plugin lock refresh, while compatibility and supply-chain acceptance stay with maintainers.; Needs attention: The diff deliberately changes the default dependency source and plugin trust boundary, so supply-chain and ownership-policy acceptance should be explicit before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Refresh runtime plugin locks during pin updates — scrip... commands.review_summary:
The PR switches the default stable gateway build to the published npm-shrinkwrapped OpenClaw package, updates pin automation/CI/Garnix/docs around that default, adds npm lock/install and build-meter scripts, and localizes the ACPX bridge under the gateway package.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: a future source bump changes sourceInfo and the wrapper lock, while the gateway asserts the generated ACPX lock version matches sourceInfo.releaseVersion. I did not run a future-release apply, but the failing path is clear in the changed scripts and derivation assertions.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the stable-pin runtime-plugin lock refresh, while compatibility and supply-chain acceptance stay with maintainers.
